title: Thelypteris pauciflora
taxonomy: Thelypteris pauciflora is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This classification places it within the larger group of Thelypteridaceae, a family of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns. The genus Thelypteris is a group of ferns that are native to North and South America. The family Thelypteridaceae is part of the order Polypodiales, which includes a wide variety of ferns. The classification of Thelypteris pauciflora is constantly being revised as new research emerges.

This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating various ailments such as wounds, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
